draft.diagnosis: The loop condition stops as soon as any odd number appears, so you never continue dividing the remaining even numbers; the intent is to keep halving while there are even numbers left, not until the first odd shows up.
draft.directive: Loop while an even value exists. Replace the current `if len([i for i in l if i%2 == 1]) == 0:`/`else: break` structure with something like `while any(i % 2 == 0 for i in l):` and keep halving inside that loop.

final.diagnosis: The loop condition stops as soon as any odd number appears, so you never continue dividing the remaining even numbers; the intent is to keep halving while there are even numbers left, not until the first odd shows up.
final.directive: Loop while an even value exists. Replace the current `if len([i for i in l if i%2 == 1]) == 0:`/`else: break` structure with something like `while any(i % 2 == 0 for i in l):` and keep halving inside that loop.

results.render: The bug is in lines 4-9 (`while True:`). The loop condition stops as soon as any odd number appears, so you never continue dividing the remaining even numbers; the intent is to keep halving while there are even numbers left, not until the first odd shows up. Loop while an even value exists. Replace the current `if len([i for i in l if i%2 == 1]) == 0:`/`else: break` structure with something like `while any(i % 2 == 0 for i in l):` and keep halving inside that loop.

draft.diagnosis: Initializing both min_H and min_W to 0 is wrong because these represent dynamic lower bounds that should start based on the initial canvas size rather than identical zero values.
draft.directive: Set min_H and min_W to the proper starting bounds derived from the input dimensions so the bounds updates work correctly.

final.diagnosis: Initializing both min_H and min_W to 0 is wrong because these represent dynamic lower bounds that should start based on the initial canvas size rather than identical zero values.
final.directive: Set min_H and min_W to the proper starting bounds derived from the input dimensions so the bounds updates work correctly.

results.render: The bug is in lines 2-3 (`min_H = 0`). Initializing both min_H and min_W to 0 is wrong because these represent dynamic lower bounds that should start based on the initial canvas size rather than identical zero values. Set min_H and min_W to the proper starting bounds derived from the input dimensions so the bounds updates work correctly.

draft.diagnosis: Subtracting 1 from the base third when choosing the height of the first cut causes you to skip the actual floor(H/3) solution and unnecessarily bias towards smaller d values, so you can miss the optimal partition.
draft.directive: Use the exact third as the base when iterating, e.g. set d = (H // 3) + i (and still guard with d > 0) so you explore the intended range around floor(H/3).

final.diagnosis: Subtracting 1 from the base third when choosing the height of the first cut causes you to skip the actual floor(H/3) solution and unnecessarily bias towards smaller d values, so you can miss the optimal partition.
final.directive: Use the exact third as the base when iterating, e.g. set d = (H // 3) + i (and still guard with d > 0) so you explore the intended range around floor(H/3).

results.render: The bug is in lines 12-16 (`d = (H // 3)- 1 + i`). Subtracting 1 from the base third when choosing the height of the first cut causes you to skip the actual floor(H/3) solution and unnecessarily bias towards smaller d values, so you can miss the optimal partition. Use the exact third as the base when iterating, e.g. set d = (H // 3) + i (and still guard with d > 0) so you explore the intended range around floor(H/3).

draft.diagnosis: Initializing count to 0 makes the loop treat every comparison as starting from scratch so when the vowel comparisons fail the reliance on count is wrong; the bug manifests before the loop even starts because count should reflect a boolean flag instead of a numeric zero.
draft.directive: Change line 3 to initialize count as False (e.g., count = False) so you simply flip it to True when a vowel is found, then test that flag instead of relying on numeric accumulation.

final.diagnosis: Initializing count to 0 makes the loop treat every comparison as starting from scratch so when the vowel comparisons fail the reliance on count is wrong; the bug manifests before the loop even starts because count should reflect a boolean flag instead of a numeric zero.
final.directive: Change line 3 to initialize count as False (e.g., count = False) so you simply flip it to True when a vowel is found, then test that flag instead of relying on numeric accumulation.

results.render: The bug is in line 3 (`count = 0`). Initializing count to 0 makes the loop treat every comparison as starting from scratch so when the vowel comparisons fail the reliance on count is wrong; the bug manifests before the loop even starts because count should reflect a boolean flag instead of a numeric zero. Change line 3 to initialize count as False (e.g., count = False) so you simply flip it to True when a vowel is found, then test that flag instead of relying on numeric accumulation.
